title: I Belong to the Rhythm of Rest
subtitle: An affirmation for settling into sleep as participation in the natural world's exhale
excerpt: A brief affirmation from Day 27 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The statement names sleep as belonging rather than withdrawal — the moment you rejoin the wider rhythm that everything else is already part of. It works as a final thought before sleep, or any moment the mind frames rest as absence.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 27 theme of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

practice.whenToUse: Use in bed as the last thing before closing your eyes, or when resistance to sleep is framed as 'I should still be doing something'.

content.text: A short affirmation from Day 27 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The statement names sleep as connection, not retreat: the moment you join the rhythm that everything living is already in. It is most useful when the mind frames rest as absence — the thing that takes you out of life rather than the thing that keeps you in it.
Use it lying down, eyes closed or nearly closed. One slow breath, then say the statement quietly three times.
I belong to the rhythm of rest. Sleep connects me to the world, not away from it. I close my eyes and join what everything else is already doing.
The 'join what everything else is already doing' line is the weight-bearing one — it shifts sleep from an individual act to participation in something already in motion.
Adapted from the Day 27 theme of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
